title: System Integration
subtitle: 70 questions covering ESB, testing & validation, performance, scalability, and ASP.NET MVC best practices.

questions.t: An operation in which a message cannot be processed due to some error that cannot be resolved.
Protocol is a changing the _____ method so two systems can interact.
An order transaction in which the message was removed from the queue or subscription and store it separately until it is ready to be processed.
It verifies the identity of applications or users sending/receiving messages.
A tool for facilitating asynchronous communication between different systems. They will keep the messages in their memory until the receiving system is ready to process them.

During message processing in an ESB, a student enrollment message fails due to a schema mismatch. The ESB retries the message but eventually places it in a dead-letter queue. Meanwhile, administrators receive an alert showing a spike in failed messages. Which ESB functions are being demonstrated?
These are intermediaries that are in charge of receiving, storing, and transmitting, communications between various computer systems.
Compare orchestration and choreography in integration. Which statement is correct?
choices: Orchestration is decentralized, choreography is centralized
Both orchestration and choreography require a single orchestrator
Orchestration uses a central controller, choreography is event-driven
Choreography cannot be used in microservices
questions.a: Orchestration uses a central controller, choreography is event-driven

questions.t: An order transaction that indicates that the message should be made available for another receiver to process.
It ensures only permitted services can access certain data or functions.
It confirms message integrity and prevents tampering.
_____ is a decentralized approach where each service knows when to act and how to interact with others, based on agreed rules.

An ASP.NET Core MVC application sends student enrollment data in JSON over HTTP. The receiving legacy system only accepts XML messages over JMS. Which ESB functions are required to enable communication between the two systems?
Service Orchestration is a _____ control mechanism where a single process (orchestrator) manages the sequence and logic of service interactions.

An enterprise service bus (ESB) is handling sensitive student enrollment data. Which combination of security mechanisms ensures that only trusted applications can access the data, the data remains confidential during transmission, and its integrity is preserved?
choices: Authentication, Authorization, Encryption, and Digital Signatures
questions.a: Authentication, Authorization, Encryption, and Digital Signatures

questions.t: An ASP.NET Core MVC app needs to send enrollment data to a legacy ERP system that only accepts XML. Which function is most relevant?
It handles the task of delivering and routing messages by using predetermined rules of policies in order to determine where incoming communications are to be sent.
Monitoring is a continuous _____ of ESB activity to ensure smooth operations and detect issues early.
Why is ESB important in enterprise systems?
choices: It replaces databases across connected application components
It ensures applications can communicate across connected application components
It eliminates the need for operating systems across connected application components
It only stores files for applications connected application components
questions.a: It ensures applications can communicate across connected application components

questions.t: Mediation is the ESB's ability to _____, enrich, or adapt messages so that different systems can understand them.
Ensure that any modification made do not result in the introduction of new _____ on the integrated system by performing _____ and _____.
A kind of validation techniques which operate across multiple servers or regions to ensure seamless operations.
Capacity testing evaluates the minimum number of users or transactions that the system can handles efficiently.
A kind of validation techniques which simulates expected user traffic to measure system performance under normal conditions.
Testing involves running the software under specific conditions to identify _____, ensure _____, and validate that it meets user _____.
It involves running the software under specific conditions to identify bugs, ensure functionality, and validate that it meets user requirements.
_____ verify the system's ability to handle increased load or user demand without degradation.
A type of integration testing which focuses on verifying the integration and data flow between various integrated systems or components.
_____ ensure the system meets acceptable response time, throughput, and resource utilization.
Under what guideline of SIT involves creating a timetable for when the testing will be carried out.
choices: Specify the condition that will allow acceptance
Employ a Number of Distinct Methods of Testing

questions.t: Manual Testing allows rapid execution of test cases.
System Integration Testing is to ensure that subsystem function harmoniously as a _____ system.
It is unnecessary to keep record of any problems that are found during testing or validation.
Capacity testing test systems that operate across multiple servers or regions to ensure seamless operation.
A type of integration testing which ensures that an entire application, from start to finish, works as expected.
End-to-end testing simulates real user scenarios by testing complete workflows across _____ components, or environments to validate the application's functionality, performance, and reliability.
Acceptance testing ensures that the system satisfies the requirements of the relevant stakeholders, which include customers.
Integration Flow Validation Techniques includes techniques for validating, routing, rules, choking the sequence in which messages are processed, and verifying message transformation.
Testing refers to the process of _____ and verifying software application or system performs as intended.
Which of the following best describes the difference between testing and validation?
choices: Validation focuses on finding bugs, while testing focuses on user satisfaction
Testing and validation are exactly the same process
Testing is only done after deployment, while validation is done before development
Testing ensures the software is built correctly, while validation ensures the right software is built correctly
questions.a: Testing ensures the software is built correctly, while validation ensures the right software is built correctly

questions.t: A kind of validation techniques pushes the system beyond its limits to identify breaking points and assess recovery.
System integration focuses on verifying the interaction and data _____ between various integrated systems or components.
A type of integration testing where individual components or units of code are tested specifically to ensure their compatibility with other units during integration.
Request validation is disabled by default to block unsafe content, enabling it when necessary helps improve flexibility without compromising your MVC best practices.
_____ scaling is also known as scaling up.
Caching involves distributing incoming traffic across multiple servers to ensure no single server becomes overwhelmed.
Incorporating Data Annotations means data validation.
_____ refers to how efficiently the integrated systems operate when performing task, processing data or responding to user request.
Caching public pages contain confidential information to boost overall application performance.
Leveraging Master Pages in ASP.NET MVC is unnecessary because each content page should define its own layout to maintain flexibility.
Asynchronous messaging enables services to communicate waiting for immediate responses.
Event-driven architecture reacts to events, triggering actions across multiple components.
Extension methods in ASP.NET MVC allow developers to write more concise and manageable LINQ queries, improving both performance and scalability.
Tuning involves tracking system metrics(CPU usage, memory consumption, response times) to ensure applications run efficiently.
Caching involves storing infrequently accessed data in memory to reduce the time and resources required for future requests.
In ASP.NET MVC, Master Pages help keep the design and layout consistent across all pages, making the application easier to manage and giving users a uniform experience.
Implementing required and length validations is considered a best practice to achieve maximum performance.
_____ refers to the ability of a system to handle increasing amounts of workload, data, process, or processes efficiently as demand grows.
Horizontal scaling approach helps in distributing the workload across _____ resources and enables the system to handle increased demands.
Horizontal scaling involves adding more _____ of resources such as servers or virtual machines to increase its processing power.

Caching relatively stale data and rendered actions of frequently changing web pages in an ASP.NET MVC application helps reduce server-client bandwidth usage and significantly improves application performance.
Repository pattern used to separate data access logic from controllers.
Scalability ensures that as your business grows, the integrated systems can manage the additional load without _____ performance or requiring significant changes to the architecture.
Vertical scaling is adding more _____ resources to an existing node.
The use of (dot) operator belongs to what practice to achieve maximum performance.
Diagonal scaling is a combination of both _____ and _____ scaling where resources are added both horizontally or vertically.
Performance measures the responsiveness, _____ and resource utilization of the system as different components interact and exchange data.
_____ scaling is also known as scaling out.
In ASP.NET MVC, model binding automatically takes data from a form (HTML input) and converts it into a C# object.
Vertical scaling is a common practice in _____ system, which make it feasible to dynamically increase the amount of system resources (ex. CPU, RAM, or storage).
